evals.prompt: Write review guidance for a new AbstractValidator<CustomerRequest> that checks required fields, email format, numeric ranges, nested address validation, and collection items. Choose built-in validators where possible and identify the package files to consult.
assertions: Prefers built-in validators before Must, Custom, or reusable custom validators.
Routes nested validators and collection rules through foundation composition guidance.
Keeps guidance specific to FluentValidation instead of broad .NET validation advice.

evals.prompt: Review an ASP.NET Core endpoint that calls Validate instead of ValidateAsync even though one FluentValidation rule queries a database asynchronously, then maps failures to ValidationProblem. Explain the safe fix and the companion skill boundary.
assertions: Requires ValidateAsync and ambient CancellationToken when any rule or dependency is async.
Keeps FluentValidation-specific rule behavior in this skill and leaves general API host shape to martix-dotnet-csharp or martix-fastendpoints.
Recommends validator tests with FluentValidation.TestHelper and boundary tests only when framework wiring is part of the contract.

evals.prompt: Plan a FluentValidation 11 to 12 upgrade for a solution with shared validators, localized messages, ASP.NET Core integration, async rules, and hundreds of tests. Classify model tier and split the work into parallel-safe slices.
assertions: Treats the initial upgrade plan as premium or mixed tier because it crosses packages and runtime behavior.
Splits validators, integration, localization, and test updates into separate worktree-safe slices.
Uses the upgrade baseline, breaking changes history, and compatibility matrix before editing.
